title: Caregiver Account Type Selection - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Caregiver Account Type Selection feature on the AT/AD platform. This feature enables users to select 'Caregiver' as their account type during sign-up, routing them to the correct onboarding flow (sign-in for existing users, registration for new users). The test plan covers account type display, selection behavior, routing logic, session persistence, dark mode support, and static content validation.

inScope: Account type options display on sign-up page
Caregiver selection routing to sign-in or registration
All four account type options visibility (PwD, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner)
Role persistence in session and analytics
Static card 'Why choose the Portal' display
Account type switching mid-flow with form reset and warning
Page refresh retaining selected role with active session
outOfScope: Caregiver registration form details (covered in SCRUM-357)
PwD registration by caregiver (covered in SCRUM-281)

testCases.title: Verify all four account type options are displayed on sign-up page
testCases.preconditions: User is not logged in; Sign-up page is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to the AT/AD platform home page
2. Click on 'Sign Up' button/link
3. Verify the sign-up page loads successfully
4. Locate the account type selection section
5. Verify 'Person with Disability' option is displayed
6. Verify 'Caregiver' option is displayed
7. Verify 'Donor' option is displayed
8. Verify 'Assistive Partner' option is displayed
9. Verify all four options are visually distinct and selectable
expectedResults: Sign-up page loads without errors
Account type selection section is clearly visible
All four account type options are displayed: Person with Disability, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner
Each option is visually distinct with clear labels
No account type is pre-selected by default (or default is clearly indicated)
Options are interactive and clickable
testCases.postconditions: User is on sign-up page with all account type options visible

testCases.title: Verify selecting Caregiver routes new user to Caregiver Registration
testCases.preconditions: User is not logged in; User does not have an existing caregiver account; Sign-up page is displayed with account type options
steps: 1. Navigate to the sign-up page
2. Locate the account type selection options
3. Click on 'Caregiver' account type option
4. Verify visual feedback indicates Caregiver is selected (highlight, border, checkmark)
5. Click 'Continue' or 'Sign Up' button to proceed
6. Verify the user is routed to the Caregiver Registration page
7. Verify the registration form is specific to Caregiver role
8. Verify the page title or heading indicates Caregiver Registration
expectedResults: Caregiver option shows selected state (visual indicator)
Clicking continue/sign-up navigates to Caregiver Registration page
Registration page is specific to Caregiver onboarding flow
Page heading or breadcrumb confirms Caregiver Registration
No errors or unexpected redirects occur
testCases.postconditions: New user is on Caregiver Registration page ready to fill details

testCases.title: Verify selecting Caregiver routes existing user to Caregiver Sign-In
testCases.preconditions: A caregiver account already exists in the system; User is not logged in; Sign-up page is displayed
steps: 1. Navigate to the sign-up page
2. Select 'Caregiver' account type
3. Click 'Continue' or proceed button
4. If the flow presents a 'Already have an account? Sign In' option, click it
5. Verify the user is routed to Caregiver Sign-In page
6. Verify the sign-in page is specific to Caregiver role
7. Verify email and password fields are present
8. Enter existing caregiver credentials and sign in
9. Verify successful login redirects to Caregiver Dashboard
expectedResults: Existing user can navigate to Caregiver Sign-In from sign-up flow
Sign-in page is specific to Caregiver role
Email and password fields are functional
Successful login redirects to Caregiver Dashboard
Session is established with Caregiver role
testCases.postconditions: Existing caregiver is logged in and on Caregiver Dashboard

testCases.title: Verify 'Why choose the Portal' static card is displayed as per design
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page with account type options visible
steps: 1. Navigate to the sign-up page
2. Locate the 'Why choose the Portal' static card/section
3. Verify the card is visible on the page
4. Verify the card content matches the design specification
5. Verify the card layout, typography, and spacing match design
6. Verify the card is positioned correctly relative to account type options
7. Verify any images or icons in the card render correctly
8. Verify the card is readable and not truncated
expectedResults: 'Why choose the Portal' static card is visible on sign-up page
Card content matches the approved design specification
Layout, typography, and spacing are consistent with design
Card is properly positioned and does not overlap other elements
All images/icons within the card render correctly
Card content is fully readable without truncation
testCases.postconditions: Static card is verified to match design specifications

testCases.title: Verify sign-up page and account type selection work in Dark Mode
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page; Dark mode toggle is available
steps: 1. Navigate to the sign-up page in default (light) mode
2. Toggle to Dark Mode using the platform's dark mode switch
3. Verify the sign-up page renders correctly in dark mode
4. Verify all four account type options are visible and readable in dark mode
5. Verify text contrast meets readability standards in dark mode
6. Select 'Caregiver' option and verify selection indicator is visible in dark mode
7. Verify 'Why choose the Portal' static card renders correctly in dark mode
8. Verify buttons and interactive elements are visible and functional in dark mode
9. Proceed through the flow and verify subsequent pages also support dark mode
expectedResults: Sign-up page renders correctly in dark mode without visual artifacts
All account type options are visible with proper contrast in dark mode
Text is readable against dark background
Selection indicator (highlight/border) is visible in dark mode
'Why choose the Portal' card renders correctly in dark mode
Buttons and links are visible and functional
No elements are invisible or unreadable due to dark mode colors
testCases.postconditions: Dark mode rendering is verified for sign-up page

testCases.title: Verify user can switch account type mid-flow with form reset and warning
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page; User has selected Caregiver and started filling registration form
steps: 1. Navigate to sign-up page
2. Select 'Caregiver' account type
3. Proceed to registration form
4. Fill in some registration fields (e.g., name, email)
5. Navigate back to account type selection
6. Select a different account type (e.g., 'Person with Disability')
7. Verify a warning message is displayed about form reset
8. Confirm the account type switch
9. Verify the form is reset and previous data is cleared
10. Verify the user is routed to the correct flow for the new account type
expectedResults: Warning message is displayed when switching account type mid-flow
Warning clearly states that form data will be reset
On confirming switch, form data is cleared completely
User is routed to the correct onboarding flow for the new account type
No residual data from previous selection remains
If user cancels the switch, original selection and data are preserved
testCases.postconditions: User is on the new account type's onboarding flow with a clean form

testCases.title: Verify page refresh retains selected role when session is active
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page; User has selected Caregiver account type; Session is active
steps: 1. Navigate to sign-up page
2. Select 'Caregiver' account type
3. Verify Caregiver is visually selected
4. Refresh the page (F5 or browser refresh)
5. Verify the page reloads successfully
6. Check if 'Caregiver' is still selected after refresh
7. Verify the user can continue the flow without re-selecting
8. If session is not active, verify the selection is not retained
expectedResults: Page refresh does not cause errors or blank page
If session is active, 'Caregiver' selection is retained after refresh
User can continue the onboarding flow without re-selecting account type
If session is not active, selection is cleared and user must re-select
testCases.postconditions: Selected role is retained (or appropriately cleared) after page refresh

testCases.title: Verify selecting each non-Caregiver account type routes correctly
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page with all account type options visible
steps: 1. Navigate to sign-up page
2. Select 'Person with Disability' option
3. Click continue/proceed
4. Verify routing to PwD onboarding flow
5. Navigate back to sign-up page
6. Select 'Donor' option
7. Click continue/proceed
8. Verify routing to Donor onboarding flow
9. Navigate back to sign-up page
10. Select 'Assistive Partner' option
11. Click continue/proceed
12. Verify routing to Assistive Partner onboarding flow
expectedResults: Person with Disability selection routes to PwD registration/sign-in flow
Donor selection routes to Donor registration/sign-in flow
Assistive Partner selection routes to AP registration/sign-in flow
Each account type routes to its specific onboarding flow
No cross-routing or incorrect flow assignment occurs
testCases.postconditions: All account type routing is verified to be correct

testCases.title: Verify user cannot proceed without selecting an account type
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page; No account type is selected
steps: 1. Navigate to sign-up page
2. Do NOT select any account type
3. Attempt to click 'Continue' or 'Sign Up' button
4. Verify the system prevents navigation without selection
5. Verify an error message or visual cue prompts user to select an account type
6. Select 'Caregiver' account type
7. Verify the continue button becomes active/enabled
8. Click continue and verify successful navigation
expectedResults: Continue/Sign Up button is disabled or shows error when no account type is selected
Error message or visual cue indicates account type selection is required
User cannot proceed to any onboarding flow without selecting an account type
After selecting an account type, the continue button becomes active
Successful navigation occurs after selection
testCases.postconditions: Validation for mandatory account type selection is verified

testCases.title: Verify sign-up page loads correctly when accessed directly via URL
testCases.preconditions: User is not logged in; Direct URL to sign-up page is known
steps: 1. Open browser and enter the direct sign-up page URL
2. Verify the page loads without errors
3. Verify all four account type options are displayed
4. Verify 'Why choose the Portal' static card is visible
5. Select 'Caregiver' and proceed
6. Verify routing works correctly from direct URL access
expectedResults: Sign-up page loads correctly from direct URL
All account type options are displayed
Static card is visible
Selection and routing work correctly
No authentication errors or redirects to unexpected pages
testCases.postconditions: Direct URL access to sign-up page is verified

AC1_account_type_options_display.description: User can see account type options: Person with Disability, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner

AC2_caregiver_routing_signin.description: Selecting Caregiver routes existing user to Caregiver Sign-in

AC3_caregiver_routing_registration.description: Selecting Caregiver routes new user to Caregiver Registration

AC5_static_card_design.description: Static card on Why choose the Portal should be as per the design

AC6_dark_mode.description: Available in Dark mode as well

EC1_switch_account_type_midflow.description: User switches account type mid-flow → form resets with warning

EC2_page_refresh_retains_role.description: Page refresh retains selected role (if session active)

assumptions: Sign-up page is accessible at the QA environment URL
All four account types (PwD, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner) are enabled in QA
Dark mode toggle is available on the sign-up page
Session management is functional in QA environment
Design specifications for 'Why choose the Portal' card are finalized
Caregiver registration flow (SCRUM-357) is at least partially functional for routing verification
dependencies: SCRUM-357: Caregiver Registration flow must be available for routing verification
SCRUM-360: Caregiver Sign-In flow must be available for existing user routing
Dark mode feature must be implemented platform-wide

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is there a default pre-selected account type, or must the user explicitly select one?
How does the system differentiate between new and existing users after Caregiver selection?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_ACCT_SEL_002 and TC_ACCT_SEL_003 routing logic

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is the 'Why choose the Portal' card the same for all account types or does it change based on selection?

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_ACCT_SEL_001, 002, 003) should be executed first as they cover core functionality
High priority tests (005, 006, 007) cover static content, dark mode, and edge cases
Medium priority tests (008, 009, 010) cover persistence, other account types, and validation
Low priority tests (013) cover direct URL access

estimated_effort.critical_path: 4-6 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
Pre-registered caregiver account for existing user tests

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria and edge cases covered
